title: Node File Services, Object Storage, and CDN Architecture · Inference Brief: Origin Path Selection
summary: Predict unknown input, typed state, command, stream, promise, storage, cache, security, cost, and recovery outcomes before reviewing Node storage-architecture decision record; use a typed control plane separated from bounded direct or streamed data planes. Planned artifact: Add and verify the origin path selection increment in Node storage-architecture decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose server upload, direct upload, redirect, proxy, signed edge delivery, or public delivery from threat, transformation, audit, and performance evidence.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, separate control-plane metadata and authorization from high-volume data transfer while preserving one auditable decision.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Origin Path Selection · Theory Origin Path Selection
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 9454 during a browser upload compatibility rollout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to select a typed control and data plane from trust, transformation, and volume evidence; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then abort during a stream or multipart promise and prove every task and part reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Origin Path Selection contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Build Origin Path Selection evidence · Theory Origin Path Selection
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: storage-ts-origin-path-selection-1u3f91" that uses case 2264 to select a typed control and data plane from trust, transformation, and volume evidence. Concept boundary: contrast one accepted path, one near miss, and the claim that still needs transfer evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node or AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, networks, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the TypeScript 7.0.2 native compiler and the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 compiler that reports 6.0.3, Node 24.18.0, AWS SDK v3.1087.0, controlled AWS, stream and handle, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.

Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose server upload, direct upload, redirect, proxy, signed edge delivery, or public delivery from threat, transformation, audit, and performance evidence.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Defend the Origin Path Selection model · Theory Origin Path Selection, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Direct-to-S3 upload always improves security because the application handles fewer bytes. Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.
